props.message:
POSTED — Guild Bylaws

1. Stalls open at first bell.
2. Coins counted twice.
3. No haggling on holy days.

(no one observes #3)

PRICES TODAY

Salt — 2 chips a measure
Knot-rope — 1 chip a fathom
Sweetwood — by the bundle
Promises — free, expire at dusk

NOTICE

Lost: a small dog answering to “Crumb.”
Reward in chips. No questions asked.

— Stallkeep Manda

WARNING

The alley past the second lamp is uneven.
The Guild is not liable for sprains, twists,
or things you tripped on while cursing the Guild.

POSTED

Pickpockets shall be named on the bell-board
for a turn of seven days, then forgiven.
The board has names on it. So does the wall behind it.

INSCRIPTION (worn)

“Trade is the smaller breath of Lirien —
shorter, but it carries grain.”

ANNOUNCEMENT

The Old Quarter scales were re-cut at the spring forge.
If yours weighs short, that is between you and your conscience.

props.open_message: Eight pebbles. Someone's slingshot supply, neatly stacked.
A small iron key. The Binds favour locks; this opens one of theirs.
A bright spark of heart. The kind merchants quietly tuck in for slow days.
A glass bottle, a small light inside. Sealed properly. Sold honest.

root.speaker: Manda
root.text: Look at you. Light pouch, heavy step. Tell me what you're after and I'll tell you what it costs.

sell.speaker: Manda
sell.text: Today? Nothing. Tomorrow? Whatever you'd pay for. Come back tomorrow.

brows.speaker: Manda
brows.text: Browse, then. The shelves are on me. The looking, half-price.

flirt.speaker: Manda
flirt.text: Don't be. I flirt with the till too. ...Mostly the till.

root.speaker: Bureaucrat Imon
root.text: *(unrolls a scroll)* Bylaw nine, subsection three: ‘No coin shall be cast in anger.’ ...And yet, every market-day...

another.speaker: Bureaucrat Imon
another.text: Bylaw twelve: ‘All wagers shall be witnessed by a sober party.’ The Guild keeps no record of a sober party in the Binds.

another2.speaker: Bureaucrat Imon
another2.text: Bylaw seventeen, omnibus: ‘Honesty is best. Where impractical, courtesy.’ My favourite, in the dignified sense.

follow.speaker: Bureaucrat Imon
follow.text: Follow them? Dear walker, the bylaws are not for following. They are for *citing*.

root.speaker: Trader Joss
root.text: Walker. You've a foreign cut to your cloak. I knew a man with that cut once, down at the Forge.

forge.speaker: Trader Joss
forge.text: Aye. Been once. The hammers there don't sound like our hammers. That's all I'll say.

forge_more.speaker: Trader Joss
forge_more.text: Hot. Loud. Old in a way our stones aren't old. I came back lighter than I went, and richer for it. ...Don't ask the way. I won't tell.

trade.speaker: Trader Joss
trade.text: What I can carry. What folk will buy. The usual quiet wares. Nothing the Guild needs to fuss over.

root.speaker: Trader Kel
root.text: Step UP, walker, step UP! Bottled birdsong! Salt that remembers its sea! A coin polished by a passing breath of Lirien!

bird.speaker: Trader Kel
bird.text: Genuine. Mostly. Caught fresh at dawn, bottled by mid-morning, marked down by lunch.

coin.speaker: Trader Kel
coin.text: She passed by, didn't notice me — but the breath caught the coin as she went. You can FEEL the polish, walker. Three chips.

root.speaker: Rin
root.text: ...You spoke first. Most don't. Most cross the alley.

alright.speaker: Rin
alright.text: Better than I deserve. I lifted purses for a turn of years. I gave it up. The Guild doesn't know yet. I'll tell them. ...In the next breath.

why.speaker: Rin
why.text: I lifted from a Sharer once. He smiled when he found out. Said the loss was a poem he hadn't written yet. I haven't slept right since.

bye.speaker: Rin
bye.text: Walk well. Mind your purse, all the same — habit's hard to lose.
